metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC1's 'at/before the anchor is grandfathered' to 'strictly before the anchor is grandfathered; the anchor commit itself is enforced' — aligning the criterion with the actual `git merge-base --is-ancestor` semantics — and confirm the boundary-selection procedure explicitly requires that no unsigned events exist IN the anchor commit itself.
evidence: AC1: 'every event at/before the anchor is grandfathered'
`_is_enforced` line 280: `return proc.returncode == 0` where the command is `git merge-base --is-ancestor <since_ref> <commit_sha>` — exits 0 when since_ref==commit_sha (commit is its own ancestor)
verify_authorship.py:507: `grandfathered = not enforced` — so AT-boundary events become `grandfathered: false`

The boundary-selection procedure (take tickets HEAD after signing is live) sidesteps this in practice, but the AC language 'at/before' is semantically wrong and will mislead a test author who builds a fixture where the boundary commit itself contains an unsigned event
findings.finding: AC1 states 'every event at/before the anchor is grandfathered', but the code's `_is_enforced` logic enforces events AT the boundary commit (because `git merge-base --is-ancestor X X` exits 0 — a commit is its own ancestor, so `since_ref == commit_sha` → ENFORCED). The correct semantic is: events STRICTLY BEFORE the anchor are grandfathered; the anchor commit itself and all descendants are enforced. The AC language 'at/before' inverts the boundary-commit treatment.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC1 ('The chosen anchor grandfathers all pre-boundary history')
scenarios: A test author following AC1 literally creates an unsigned event in the exact boundary commit and expects it to be grandfathered — but the code enforces it, making the test assertion wrong.

If the chosen anchor commit itself happens to contain any unsigned event (e.g., a race between signing infrastructure coming live and the first signed write), the gate will fail even though the AC says it should be grandfathered.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, replace 'every event at/before the anchor is grandfathered' with 'every event whose introducing commit is a STRICT ancestor of the anchor is grandfathered (the anchor commit itself is enforced)'. In the boundary-selection procedure, add: after `rebar verify-identity --all --require-authenticated --since <candidate>` exits 0, that already confirms no events at/after the candidate are unsigned-and-enforced, so the chosen candidate is safe — but document explicitly that unsigned events introduced IN the candidate commit would be enforced.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `test_identity_enforce_since_<slug>.py` throughout the plan body (What, AC2 proving command, Scope) with the concrete filename `test_identity_enforce_since_grandfather.py` so the AC proving command is executable verbatim.
evidence: Plan body: 'tests/unit/test_identity_enforce_since_<slug>.py' (used in What, AC2, and Scope)
Ticket file_impact metadata: 'tests/unit/test_identity_enforce_since_grandfather.py, reason: the new regression test'
AC2 proving command: 'pytest tests/unit/test_identity_enforce_since_<slug>.py -q exits 0' — not executable as written
findings.finding: The plan's `file_impact` metadata (in the ticket, shown as 'test_identity_enforce_since_grandfather.py') names a concrete slug, but the plan body consistently uses the placeholder `test_identity_enforce_since_<slug>.py` including in the AC proving command. If an implementer reads only the plan body, the test filename is unresolved, and the AC proving command (`pytest tests/unit/test_identity_enforce_since_<slug>.py -q`) cannot be executed verbatim.
findings.location: What section / AC2 proving command / Scope section
scenarios: Implementer creates a file named literally test_identity_enforce_since_<slug>.py, diverging from the ticket's file_impact.
AC2's proving command cannot be copy-pasted to verify the test passes without substituting the slug.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace all occurrences of `test_identity_enforce_since_<slug>.py` in the plan body with `tests/unit/test_identity_enforce_since_grandfather.py`, matching the `file_impact` entry already recorded in the ticket metadata.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve `<slug>` to a concrete filename (e.g. `test_identity_enforce_since_8d91.py`, derived from the ticket short-ID `8d91`) in both the What section and the AC2 proving command, eliminating the executor-decided placeholder.
evidence: Plan text: 'pytest tests/unit/test_identity_enforce_since_<slug>.py -q exits 0'
The `<slug>` token appears in both the What section and the AC2 proving command without being resolved to a concrete value.
Defect taxonomy #4 (fixture invalidity): a path that may not exist cannot exercise the claim.
findings.finding: The test filename and its pytest proving command both contain `<slug>` as an unresolved placeholder, deferring the concrete file name to the executor. If the executor picks a name that differs from what the proving command assumes, `pytest tests/unit/test_identity_enforce_since_<slug>.py -q` targets a non-existent path; pytest behavior on a missing path varies by version (exit code 4 in some, 0 in others), potentially making the AC silently pass or fail for the wrong reason.
scenarios: Executor names the file `test_identity_enforce_since_8d91.py`; the literal proving command (with `<slug>`) collects 0 tests; on pytest ≥7 this exits with code 5 (error), on older versions it may exit 0 — either way the AC is not reliably verified.
Reviewer runs the proving command verbatim with `<slug>` still in it and gets a confusing result.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace every occurrence of `<slug>` with `8d91` (the first segment of the ticket ID `8d91-5103-ae1b-44a7`), giving the canonical path `tests/unit/test_identity_enforce_since_8d91.py` and the proving command `pytest tests/unit/test_identity_enforce_since_8d91.py -q`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit prerequisite or 'blocked-by 79da, 4dc0, 1a51' note to AC1 (or to an 'ACs unlock when' preamble) so the proving command is only run after all writers are confirmed signing.
evidence: What section: 'Method: after those three stories confirm signing, take a candidate anchor = current tickets HEAD and verify rebar verify-identity --all --require-authenticated --since <candidate> exits 0'
AC1 text references no prerequisite state or dependency on 79da/4dc0/1a51 being complete
ABSENCE: No 'depends-on' or 'blocked-by' field anywhere in the plan; ACs are written as if self-contained
findings.finding: AC1's proving command (`rebar verify-identity --all --require-authenticated --since <anchor>` exits 0) can only be meaningfully executed AFTER stories 79da, 4dc0, and 1a51 confirm that all writers are signing — but this blocking dependency is not stated in the ACs, creating a gap between the What section's procedural sequencing and the acceptance criteria.
findings.location: What section ('after those three stories confirm signing, take a candidate anchor') vs AC1 ('exits 0 — zero enforced, non-verified events')
scenarios: An implementer attempts to choose the anchor and validate AC1 before 79da/4dc0/1a51 are done; the command exits non-zero due to unsigned CI/Gerrit events; the implementer wrongly concludes the grandfathering logic is broken rather than that the prerequisite state hasn't been reached.
findings.suggested_fix: Prepend to AC1: 'Prerequisite: stories 79da, 4dc0, and 1a51 must be merged and signing confirmed live across all writers before this AC can be verified. Once confirmed:' then continue with the existing proving command.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Retitle or add a one-line clarification note in the title/Why section distinguishing 'decide and document the first anchor value' from 'repin an existing value' to prevent reader confusion about the deliverable scope.
evidence: Title: 'Verify and repin identity.enforce_since so pre-flip events are grandfathered'
Plan body: 'the atomic-flip story 8d39 writes enforce_since exactly once, so there is no double-write'
Scope: 'Writing enforce_since into rebar.toml and the flag flip both belong to 8d39 — this story does not modify rebar.toml'
ABSENCE: no prior enforce_since value is mentioned as existing in rebar.toml
findings.finding: The title says 'repin identity.enforce_since' which implies correcting an existing pinned value, but the plan body reveals no prior value exists — this story only decides and documents the first-ever anchor value, with the actual write deferred to story 8d39. A reader following the title alone could expect a corrective update to rebar.toml in this story.
scenarios: A developer reading only the title assigns this story expecting a concrete rebar.toml change and is confused when the deliverable is a ticket comment + test file.
During review, a reviewer flags the story as incomplete because rebar.toml is untouched, missing that the split is intentional.
findings.suggested_fix: Retitle to 'Verify grandfathering logic, decide enforce_since anchor, and add regression test' — or append a parenthetical to the Why section: '("repin" here means choosing the anchor value for the first time, not correcting an existing one)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit prerequisite (e.g. `depends_on: [79da, 4dc0, 1a51]` or a gating AC like 'Stories 79da, 4dc0, and 1a51 are merged and signing is confirmed') so the executor knows AC3 cannot begin until those stories are done.
evidence: Plan text: 'after those three stories confirm signing, take a candidate anchor = current tickets HEAD'
No `depends_on` or prerequisite gate is listed for 79da, 4dc0, or 1a51 anywhere in the plan.

F1 criterion (b): evaluable in-session — the anchor cannot be chosen until external stories confirm signing, making in-session completability conditional on story sequencing not encoded in this ticket.
findings.finding: AC3 (decide and document the enforcement-boundary anchor) is explicitly gated on three other stories completing first ('after those three stories confirm signing': 79da, 4dc0, 1a51), but no blocking prerequisite is declared on this ticket and no sequencing AC establishes that those stories are done before the anchor-selection step runs. An executor working this ticket in isolation cannot satisfy AC3 in a single session if those stories are still open.
findings.location: What section – 'Decide and document the enforcement-boundary anchor'; AC3
scenarios: An executor picks up this ticket before 79da/4dc0/1a51 are merged; the anchor-decision step cannot be completed; AC3 is blocked but the ticket carries no signal that it is waiting.
A reviewer tries to close this ticket, finds AC3 unverifiable, and cannot tell whether the story is incomplete or just waiting on dependencies.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Prerequisites' section (or a gating AC0) stating: 'This story's anchor-decision step (AC3) is unblocked only after stories 79da, 4dc0, and 1a51 are merged; AC1 and AC2 (logic verification and regression test) may proceed independently.'

findings.finding: AC3's positive claim — 'the 40-hex boundary SHA + rationale are recorded as a comment on this ticket' — has no in-session proving command; only the negative (`git diff --stat ... rebar.toml`) is verifiable in-session.
findings.suggested_fix: Prepend `[operator-attested]` to the 'anchor recorded' sentence in AC3 and specify that the done-evidence is a ticket comment containing the 40-hex SHA, so reviewers know exactly what to look for.

findings.finding: The plan's test spec says 'in-process `verify_authorship.cli`' AND 'assert per-event `grandfathered` flags', but `cli()` returns only an int (exit code). Capturing per-event grandfathered flags from an in-process call requires redirecting sys.stdout and parsing `--format json` output — a non-trivial mechanism the plan leaves unspecified. The existing comparable test (`test_identity_ac7_bff8_heldout.py`) sidesteps this by using subprocess+JSON. If the implementer naively calls `cli([...])` in-process and tries to inspect per-event flags without stdout redirection, the assertion cannot be written as described.
findings.location: What section / regression test description; AC line: 'assert per-event `grandfathered` flags in a single mixed store' and 'Uses … in-process `verify_authorship.cli`'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'in-process verify_authorship.cli' with subprocess invocation using '--format json', then parse stdout with json.loads — consistent with test_identity_ac7_bff8_heldout.py and directly assertable on per-entry 'grandfathered' fields without any stdout-capture machinery.
